tier_basis: Stratified on 4 real daily objects: VERY HIGH BTCUSDT spot (9.82 MB/d, 653k rows), HIGH perp trades (15.1 MB/d, 1.8M rows), UPPER-MID BTCUSD-2019 inverse (4.0 MB/d), LOW BTCUSDT-2020 (0.12 MB/d). U25 weighted ~60%% mid/low, 40%% high/very-high.
assumptions: Per-row bytes empirically measured (trades comp 8-15 B/row, ext 54-87 B/row). Parquet=0.5x ext. Manifest overhead 2%% excluded for clarity.

tier_basis: 1m klines BTCUSDT 2025-01-01 = 1440 rows/0.069 MB comp/day. All pairs 1440 rows/day regardless of activity.
assumptions: Bars tiny and uniform; dominant only at U100 scale.

tier_basis: Bybit funding capped <=100 recent; Binance funding monthly 94 rows/0.825KB. ~3-8 intervals/day/pair.
OKX 427 SWAP instruments ~0.44 MB one-shot; daily delta ~0.005 MB.
U50: more high/very-high pairs than U25 (~2x U25 trade volume mix).
assumptions: Scaled from U25 with measured per-pair weights; not a single BTC extrapolation.

tier_basis: U100: long tail adds many LOW/SPARSE pairs (sparse assumed ~0.02 MB/d each, flagged as not directly sampled). Median case dominated by mid/low.
assumptions: SPARSE tier inferred (0.02 MB/d) — NOT independently sampled; upper/stress cases below raise it.

tier_basis: Upper-quantile: assume U100 mix skewed to high/very-high (more BTC/ETH/ SOL-class pairs active).
assumptions: Upper-quantile assumption from measured high/very-high per-pair MB.

tier_basis: Stress: all 100 pairs at VERY HIGH (BTCUSDT-like) trade rate (worst case, not expected).
assumptions: Stress only; demonstrates upper bound if many pairs become very-high-activity.
Order ~4-7 TB canonical for trades at U100 median; bars/funding negligible.
